{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant Production Manager (Oil Palm)",
  "description": "Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client&rsquo;s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Production Manager (Oil Palm)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose


	The Assistant Production Manager supports the execution of production operations by translating production plans into daily shop-floor activities.
	The role ensures optimal utilization of manpower, machinery, and materials to achieve production targets while maintaining cost efficiency, product quality, safety compliance, and operational discipline.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement daily and shift production plans to achieve &ge;95% output targets.
	Allocate and manage manpower, machines, and materials efficiently.
	Monitor production performance and resolve deviations in real time.
	Control labor costs, overtime, and ensure payroll accuracy.
	Optimize machine utilization and reduce downtime through coordination with maintenance.
	Enforce quality standards, minimize waste, and improve first-pass yield.
	Drive cost efficiency and continuous improvement initiatives (Lean, 5S, Kaizen).
	Ensure strict adherence to safety policies and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare accurate production, labor, and performance reports.
	Supervise, coach, and enforce discipline among production teams.


Qualification and Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering or related field wit&nbsp;&nbsp;5&ndash;8 years manufacturing experience, with 2&ndash;3 years in a supervisory role.
	Strong skills in production planning, cost control, and problem-solving.
	Proficiency in ERP systems and Excel.
	Knowledge of Lean manufacturing is an advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;career.bridgemeadnghr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Assistant Production Manager (Oil Palm) Job at Bridgemead HR. and Advisory




        
    

        
        Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client’s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Production Manager (Oil Palm)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Purpose
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Assistant Production Manager supports the execution of production operations by translating production plans into daily shop-floor activities.
	The role ensures optimal utilization of manpower, machinery, and materials to achieve production targets while maintaining cost efficiency, product quality, safety compliance, and operational discipline.


Key Responsibilities


	Implement daily and shift production plans to achieve ≥95% output targets.
	Allocate and manage manpower, machines, and materials efficiently.
	Monitor production performance and resolve deviations in real time.
	Control labor costs, overtime, and ensure payroll accuracy.
	Optimize machine utilization and reduce downtime through coordination with maintenance.
	Enforce quality standards, minimize waste, and improve first-pass yield.
	Drive cost efficiency and continuous improvement initiatives (Lean, 5S, Kaizen).
	Ensure strict adherence to safety policies and regulatory requirements.
	Prepare accurate production, labor, and performance reports.
	Supervise, coach, and enforce discipline among production teams.


Qualification and Experience


	Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering or related field wit  5–8 years manufacturing experience, with 2–3 years in a supervisory role.
	Strong skills in production planning, cost control, and problem-solving.
	Proficiency in ERP systems and Excel.
	Knowledge of Lean manufacturing is an advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)",
  "description": "Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client&rsquo;s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Objective


	Lead and optimize all operational activities (plantation, milling, logistics, and supply chain) to ensure efficiency, profitability, compliance, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the MD/CEO in defining and executing strategy.
	Oversee plantation and milling operations to maximize yield, efficiency, and quality.
	Drive mechanization, modernization, and sustainability initiatives.
	Manage budgets, control costs, and track KPIs for performance improvement.
	Lead and develop teams, ensuring productivity and accountability.
	Ensure compliance with RSPO, ISO, and regulatory standards.
	Manage operational risks, stakeholder engagement, and community relations.
	Provide regular operational reports and support board/investor engagements.


Qualifications &amp; Experience


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Agriculture, Engineering, Business, or a related field (MBA preferred).
	15+ years of experience in agribusiness/plantation operations, with 5+ years in senior leadership.
	Strong experience in oil palm operations, sustainability standards, and budget management.


Professional Certifications (Preferred):


	PMP
	Lean Six Sigma
	RSPO
	ISO
	HSE (NEBOSH/IOSH)
	Relevant professional memberships


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to:&nbsp;career.bridgemeadnghr@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm) Job at Bridgemead HR. and Advisory




        
    

        
        Bridgemead HR provides customized HR solutions to optimize workforce productivity and profitability. Our expert trainers design bespoke training programs, innovative HR strategies, and efficient payroll systems tailored to each client’s unique needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Chief Operating Officer (Oil Palm)

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Objective
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Lead and optimize all operational activities (plantation, milling, logistics, and supply chain) to ensure efficiency, profitability, compliance, and sustainable growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the MD/CEO in defining and executing strategy.
	Oversee plantation and milling operations to maximize yield, efficiency, and quality.
	Drive mechanization, modernization, and sustainability initiatives.
	Manage budgets, control costs, and track KPIs for performance improvement.
	Lead and develop teams, ensuring productivity and accountability.
	Ensure compliance with RSPO, ISO, and regulatory standards.
	Manage operational risks, stakeholder engagement, and community relations.
	Provide regular operational reports and support board/investor engagements.


Qualifications & Experience


	Bachelor’s degree in Agriculture, Engineering, Business, or a related field (MBA preferred).
	15+ years of experience in agribusiness/plantation operations, with 5+ years in senior leadership.
	Strong experience in oil palm operations, sustainability standards, and budget management.


Professional Certifications (Preferred):


	PMP
	Lean Six Sigma
	RSPO
	ISO
	HSE (NEBOSH/IOSH)
	Relevant professional memberships


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their Applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

  "title": "Facility Manager",
  "description": "Taeillo is an e-commerce furniture and lifestyle brand that manufactures furniture by specializing in African-inspired designs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Facility Manager

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Job Summary


	The Facility Manager will beresponsible for overseeing the maintenance, safety, and efficiency of the company&rsquo;s manufacturing plant, warehouse, and office facilities. This role ensures that all physical assets, utilities, and support services operate smoothly to support uninterrupted furniture production and distribution.


Job Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operations of the manufacturing facility, including factory floor, warehouses, and office spaces.
	Develop and implement preventive and corrective maintenance schedules for machinery, equipment, and building infrastructure.
	Ensure proper functioning of utilities such as power supply, water systems, HVAC, and waste management.
	Coordinate repairs and maintenance of carpentry machines, upholstery equipment, and finishing tools.
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations.
	Implement workplace safety policies and conduct regular safety inspections.
	Monitor fire safety systems, emergency preparedness, and evacuation procedures.
	Investigate incidents and recommend corrective actions.
	Source, negotiate, and manage service providers (cleaning, security, maintenance, waste disposal, etc.).
	Optimize space utilization for production, storage, and office operations.
	Maintain records of company assets including machinery, tools, and facility equipment.
	Monitor energy consumption and implement cost-saving initiatives.
	Prepare and manage facility budgets, including maintenance and operational costs.
	Oversee facility security systems, access control, and surveillance.
	Ensure safeguarding of raw materials, finished goods, and equipment.
	Conduct risk assessments and implement mitigation strategies.
	Implement waste reduction and recycling initiatives (wood, foam, fabric, etc.).
	Ensure proper disposal of industrial waste and compliance with environmental standards


Qualification/Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Engineering, Facility Management, Estate Management, or a related field.
	5+ years of experience in facility management, preferably in manufacturing or industrial environments.
	Experience in furniture production or related industries is an advantage.
	Strong knowledge of building systems, maintenance, and industrial manufacturing equipment.
	Good understanding of health and safety regulations.
	Excellent problem-solving and organizational skills.
	Ability to work under pressure in a production-driven environment.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;peopleandculture@taeillo.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

	The Facility Manager will beresponsible for overseeing the maintenance, safety, and efficiency of the company’s manufacturing plant, warehouse, and office facilities. Job Responsibilities

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Head Of Department (Account)",
  "description": "Brit Properties Nigeria Limited is a property development company incorporated in Nigeria by Companies and Allied Matters Act. Our primary business interests are land, infrastructure/property development, land survey, property marketing and estate agency. We strive to provide exceptional customer service while offering our clients the best property options. We are committed to making good property decision for both existing and potential clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head Of Department (Account)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ajah, Lagos,
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Financial Leadership:


	Provide strategic financial leadership to the executive team, contributing to the overall success and growth of the company.
	Develop and implement financial policies, procedures, and controls to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and best practices.


Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Prepare and analyze financial statements, budgets, and forecasts to guide decision making and resource allocation.
	Conduct variance analysis and provide recommendations for improving financial performance.


Team Management:


	Lead and mentor the accounting team, fostering a culture of collaboration, accountability, and professional development.
	Oversee day-to-day activities of the accounting department, ensuring accuracy and timeliness of financial reporting.


Financial Reporting:


	Ensure timely and accurate preparation of financial reports for internal and external stakeholders.
	Coordinate with external auditors and regulatory authorities as required.


Cash Management:


	Manage cash flow to optimize working capital and support operational requirements.
	Implement effective treasury management practices.


Key Performance Indicators
Compliance:


	Stay updated on relevant accounting standards, tax laws, and regulatory changes, ensuring the company&#39;s compliance.
	Collaborate with legal and compliance teams to address financial and regulatory&nbsp;matters.


Financial Leadership:


	Successful implementation of financial policies and procedures.
	Contribution to the development and achievement of strategic financial goals.


Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Accuracy of financial forecasts and budgets.
	Timely identification and communication of significant variances.


Team Management:


	Employee satisfaction and engagement within the accounting team.
	Achievement of team performance targets and deadlines.


Financial Reporting:


	Timeliness and accuracy of internal and external financial reports.
	Successful completion of audits with minimal findings.


Cash Management:


	Maintenance of optimal working capital levels.
	Effectiveness in managing cash flow to meet operational needs.


Compliance:


	Adherence to accounting standards, tax regulations, and legal requirements.
	Successful resolution of financial and regulatory issues.


Qualifications


	Continuous professional development and relevant certifications.


Education and Work Experience:


	Required: Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. A master&#39;s degree or
	professional accounting qualification (e.g., ACCA, CPA) is a plus.
	Proven experience (6 years) in a senior financial management role within the real estate&nbsp;industry.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial analysis, and reporting.
	Leadership and team management experience.


Overall Performance:


	Achievement of company financial targets and objectives.
	Continuous improvement in financial processes and efficiency.


Ethical Standards:


	Adherence to high ethical standards in financial decision-making and reporting.


Competencies and Skills:


	Exceptional leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in financial management software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Effective communication and collaboration within the team.
	Utilization of financial management software and tools.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;onedibe.kosisochukwu@britproperties.ng&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",

  },

Head Of Department (Account) Job at Brit Properties Nigeria Limited




        
    

        
        Brit Properties Nigeria Limited is a property development company incorporated in Nigeria by Companies and Allied Matters Act. Our primary business interests are land, infrastructure/property development, land survey, property marketing and estate agency. We strive to provide exceptional customer service while offering our clients the best property options. We are committed to making good property decision for both existing and potential clients.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Head Of Department (Account)

Job Location: Ajah, Lagos,
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
Financial Leadership:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Provide strategic financial leadership to the executive team, contributing to the overall success and growth of the company.
	Develop and implement financial policies, procedures, and controls to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and best practices.


Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Prepare and analyze financial statements, budgets, and forecasts to guide decision making and resource allocation.
	Conduct variance analysis and provide recommendations for improving financial performance.


Team Management:


	Lead and mentor the accounting team, fostering a culture of collaboration, accountability, and professional development.
	Oversee day-to-day activities of the accounting department, ensuring accuracy and timeliness of financial reporting.


Financial Reporting:


	Ensure timely and accurate preparation of financial reports for internal and external stakeholders.
	Coordinate with external auditors and regulatory authorities as required.


Cash Management:


	Manage cash flow to optimize working capital and support operational requirements.
	Implement effective treasury management practices.


Key Performance Indicators
Compliance:


	Stay updated on relevant accounting standards, tax laws, and regulatory changes, ensuring the company's compliance.
	Collaborate with legal and compliance teams to address financial and regulatory matters.


Financial Leadership:


	Successful implementation of financial policies and procedures.
	Contribution to the development and achievement of strategic financial goals.


Financial Planning and Analysis:


	Accuracy of financial forecasts and budgets.
	Timely identification and communication of significant variances.


Team Management:


	Employee satisfaction and engagement within the accounting team.
	Achievement of team performance targets and deadlines.


Financial Reporting:


	Timeliness and accuracy of internal and external financial reports.
	Successful completion of audits with minimal findings.


Cash Management:


	Maintenance of optimal working capital levels.
	Effectiveness in managing cash flow to meet operational needs.


Compliance:


	Adherence to accounting standards, tax regulations, and legal requirements.
	Successful resolution of financial and regulatory issues.


Qualifications


	Continuous professional development and relevant certifications.


Education and Work Experience:


	Required: Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field. A master's degree or
	professional accounting qualification (e.g., ACCA, CPA) is a plus.
	Proven experience (6 years) in a senior financial management role within the real estate industry.
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles, financial analysis, and reporting.
	Leadership and team management experience.


Overall Performance:


	Achievement of company financial targets and objectives.
	Continuous improvement in financial processes and efficiency.


Ethical Standards:


	Adherence to high ethical standards in financial decision-making and reporting.


Competencies and Skills:


	Exceptional leadership and team management skills.
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving abilities.
	Proficiency in financial management software and Microsoft Office Suite.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.
	Effective communication and collaboration within the team.
	Utilization of financial management software and tools.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Position: Account Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	
	The Account Officer is responsible for managing daily financial transactions, keeping accurate records, and supporting the finance department to ensure proper financial management of the company.
	


Key Responsibilities


	Record daily financial transactions (income and expenses)
	Prepare and maintain accurate financial records
	Handle invoices, receipts, and payments
	Reconcile bank statements and company accounts
	Assist in preparing financial reports (weekly/monthly)
	Monitor cash flow and report any issues
	Work with other departments on financial matters
	Ensure compliance with company financial policies.


KPIs (Performance Measurement)


	Accuracy of financial records
	Timeliness of reports
	Proper documentation of transactions
	Error rate in financial entries
	Compliance with financial procedures.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
	1&ndash;2 years of experience in accounting or similar role
	Basic knowledge of accounting principles
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Excel and accounting software
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Good organizational skills.


Key Skills:


	Numerical and analytical skills
	Attention to detail
	Time management
	Integrity and confidentiality
	Communication skills.


Method of Application
